description: The longest ancient beam-style stone bridge in China, renowned as 'No bridge under heaven is longer than this one,' a marvel of ancient engineering.
An important Mazu worship site along China's southeastern coast, featuring ancient architecture and thriving incense offerings.
Ruins of an ancient city gate from the Song and Yuan dynasties, bearing witness to the historical development of Quanzhou and holding high archaeological value.
Remnants of the southern gate of the ancient city from the Song and Yuan dynasties, showcasing the historical changes of Quanzhou.
A coastal area with a seaside boardwalk, a premium seaside leisure destination near Quanzhou.
The largest comprehensive park in Quanzhou city, featuring picturesque lakes and mountains, ideal for leisurely strolls.
A quintessential example of Minnan red-brick mansions, a masterpiece of overseas Chinese architectural art from the Qing dynasty.
Showcasing the historical and cultural ties between Fujian and Taiwan, offering insights into the development of cross-strait exchanges.
The largest Buddhist temple in Fujian Province, with the famous East and West Twin Pagodas serving as landmarks of Quanzhou's ancient city.
The most historically rich ancient street in Quanzhou, preserving traditional Minnan architectural styles and offering a variety of local delicacies.
A popular cultural and creative park, blending traditional architecture with modern cultural and creative features, a must-visit spot.
Well-preserved alleys of traditional Minnan residential buildings, showcasing the living atmosphere of Quanzhou's ancient city.
A Ming dynasty garrison town, retaining ancient city walls and traditional streets, with a tranquil and charming night view.
A modern seaside leisure area in Quanzhou, perfect for strolling and relaxing while enjoying the ocean view.
The most popular folk belief site in Quanzhou, dedicated to Guan Yu and Yue Fei, featuring exquisite architectural carvings.
A seaside Taoist temple dedicated to the True Warrior Emperor, an excellent spot for sea-viewing and blessings, with beautiful coastal scenery.
One of the oldest surviving Islamic mosques in China, reflecting the multicultural integration of Quanzhou.
